cards.back: A one-way mathematical function that takes input data of any size and produces a fixed-size string of characters, known as a hash or digest.
A distributed digital ledger technology that records transactions and data across a network of computers in a secure and transparent manner.
The process by which new cryptocurrency coins are created and added to a blockchain, typically through a competitive process that involves solving complex mathematical problems.
A mechanism used to validate transactions and ensure the accuracy of a blockchain, such as Proof of Work (PoW) or Proof of Stake (PoS).
A self-executing program that automates the enforcement and execution of an agreement or contract between parties, using blockchain technology.
